missing_data.label: Missing Data
missing_data.summary: Learn what `NULL` means, how to use `IS NULL` and `IS NOT NULL`, and how to avoid common mistakes when checking missing values.

is_not_null.title: How does IS NOT NULL work?

what_null_means.title: `NULL` means the value is missing or unknown.
what_null_means.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, `NULL` does not mean the same thing as empty text or zero.

It means:

- the value is missing
- the value is unknown
- the value is not currently stored

Code example:

~~~sql
SELECT full_name, email
FROM customers
WHERE email IS NULL;
~~~

This keeps rows where the email field has no stored value.

So the big idea is:

> `NULL` means no actual value is present there

### Try it yourself

Look at these three ideas:

- `NULL`
- empty text
- zero

Ask yourself:

- which one means no stored value?
- why are they not all the same thing?
- what kind of field might realistically contain `NULL`?

is_null.title: Use `IS NULL` when rows with missing values are needed.
is_null.bodyMarkdown:
To find missing data, SQL uses `IS NULL`.

This is different from exact matching with `=`.

Code example:

~~~sql
SELECT full_name, city
FROM customers
WHERE city IS NULL;
~~~

This keeps rows where the city value is missing.

A strong idea here is:

> `IS NULL` checks for missing values directly

### Try it yourself

Read this query:

~~~sql
SELECT full_name, email
FROM customers
WHERE email IS NULL;
~~~

Ask yourself:

- what kind of rows should come back?
- which column is being checked for missing data?
- why would `= NULL` be the wrong style here?

is_not_null.title: Use `IS NOT NULL` when rows that do have a value are needed.
is_not_null.bodyMarkdown:
Sometimes the opposite of missing data is needed.

That is where `IS NOT NULL` helps.

It keeps rows where a real value is present.

Code example:

~~~sql
SELECT full_name, email
FROM customers
WHERE email IS NOT NULL;
~~~

This keeps rows where the email field contains a stored value.

So the key idea is:

> `IS NOT NULL` checks for values that exist

### Try it yourself

Compare these two checks:

- `email IS NULL`
- `email IS NOT NULL`

Ask yourself:

- which one keeps missing emails?
- which one keeps present emails?
- how do the results differ?

common_null_mistakes.title: Common NULL mistakes come from treating NULL like normal text or number data.
common_null_mistakes.bodyMarkdown:
A very common mistake is trying to compare `NULL` with `=` or `!=`.

That usually does not work the way people expect.

Useful habits are:

- use `IS NULL` for missing values
- use `IS NOT NULL` for present values
- check the result to make sure the missing-data logic worked

Code example:

~~~sql
SELECT full_name, email, city
FROM customers
WHERE email IS NULL OR city IS NULL;
~~~

This finds rows where one of those important fields is missing.

So the main idea is:

> NULL needs its own special checks

### Try it yourself

Look at this wrong idea:

~~~sql
WHERE email = NULL
~~~

Ask yourself:

- why is this the wrong style for missing data?
- what should replace it?
- what should be checked in the returned rows after fixing it?

m4_s03_find_missing_emails.title: Find missing emails
m4_s03_find_missing_emails.prompt:
The SQLite workspace already has a table named `customers`.

Write a query that shows the `full_name` and `email` columns for customers whose `email` is missing.

Then click **Run**.

help.concept: Missing values are checked with `IS NULL`, not with normal equality matching.
help.hint_1: Select `full_name` and `email`, then use the missing-value check on `email`.
help.hint_2: Build a two-column query on `customers`, then keep only rows where the email field has no stored value.

m4_s03_find_missing_emails.hint: Use `IS NULL` on the email column.

m4_s03_find_missing_cities.title: Find missing cities
m4_s03_find_missing_cities.prompt:
The `customers` table already exists.

Write a query that shows the `full_name` and `city` columns for customers whose `city` is missing.

Then run it.

help.concept: `IS NULL` is the standard way to search for missing values in a column.
help.hint_1: Select `full_name` and `city`, then check for missing values in `city`.
help.hint_2: Write a two-column query on `customers`, then keep only rows where the city field has no stored value.

m4_s03_find_missing_cities.hint: Use `IS NULL` on the city column.

m4_s03_find_present_emails.title: Find customers with emails
m4_s03_find_present_emails.prompt:
The `customers` table already exists.

Write a query that shows the `full_name` and `email` columns for customers whose `email` is present.

Then click **Run**.

help.concept: `IS NOT NULL` is used when rows that do have a value are needed.
help.hint_1: Select `full_name` and `email`, then check that the email is present.
help.hint_2: Build a two-column query on `customers`, then keep only rows where the email field has a real stored value.

m4_s03_find_present_emails.hint: Use `IS NOT NULL` on the email column.
m4_s03_find_present_emails.starterCode: -- Show customers with an email value

m4_s03_find_present_city_status.title: Find non-missing city values
m4_s03_find_present_city_status.prompt:
The `customers` table already exists.

Write a query that shows the `full_name`, `city`, and `status` columns for customers whose `city` is present.

Then run it.

help.concept: `IS NOT NULL` keeps rows where the chosen field contains a real value.
help.hint_1: Select the three requested columns, then require that `city` is present.
help.hint_2: Write a three-column query on `customers`, then keep only rows where the city field is not missing.

m4_s03_find_present_city_status.starterCode: -- Show customers whose city is present

m4_s03_find_missing_email_or_city.title: Find rows with missing data
m4_s03_find_missing_email_or_city.prompt:
The `customers` table already exists.

Write a query that shows the `full_name`, `email`, and `city` columns for customers whose `email` is missing or whose `city` is missing.

Then click **Run**.

help.concept: Missing-data checks can be combined, but each NULL check still needs the correct NULL syntax.
help.hint_1: Select the three requested columns, then write one missing-value check for `email` and another for `city`.
help.hint_2: Build a three-column query on `customers`, then keep rows where either the email field is missing or the city field is missing.

m4_s03_find_missing_email_or_city.hint: Use two NULL checks joined with OR.
m4_s03_find_missing_email_or_city.starterCode: -- Show customers missing email or city

m4_s03_missing_data_project.title: Practice missing data filters
m4_s03_missing_data_project.prompt:
Use the SQL workspace on the right to practice missing-data checks on the `customers` table.

For each task:

1. type the query in the editor
2. click **Run**
3. read the **Results** tab
4. verify whether the returned rows really have missing or present values in the target column

Tasks:

1. Show `full_name` and `email` for customers whose `email` is missing
2. Show `full_name` and `city` for customers whose `city` is missing
3. Show `full_name` and `email` for customers whose `email` is present
4. Show `full_name`, `city`, and `status` for customers whose `city` is present
5. Show `full_name`, `email`, and `city` for customers whose `email` is missing or whose `city` is missing

Run one query at a time.
This project is about using `IS NULL` and `IS NOT NULL` correctly.

help.concept: This project builds strong missing-data habits: use the correct NULL checks, avoid normal equality logic, and verify the returned rows carefully.
help.hint_1: Some tasks ask for missing values and some ask for present values. Match the SQL check to that meaning before writing the query.
help.hint_2: Before running each query, ask whether the column should be missing or present. Then use the NULL check that matches that idea.

m4_s03_missing_data_project.hint: Decide missing or present first, then choose the right NULL check.
m4_s03_missing_data_project.starterCode:
-- Task 1
-- Show customers with missing email

m4_s03_null_meaning.title: What does NULL mean?
m4_s03_null_meaning.prompt: What does `NULL` usually mean in SQL?

help.concept: `NULL` means a value is missing or unknown.
help.hint_2: It does not mean the same thing as zero or empty text.

options.a: The value is missing or unknown
options.c: The value is always empty text

m4_s03_is_null_meaning.title: What does IS NULL do?
m4_s03_is_null_meaning.prompt: What is the main job of `IS NULL` in SQL?

help.concept: `IS NULL` checks whether a field has no stored value.
help.hint_2: It is used when rows where the chosen column has no actual value stored are needed.

options.a: It checks whether a value is missing

m4_s03_is_not_null_meaning.title: What does IS NOT NULL do?
m4_s03_is_not_null_meaning.prompt: What is the main job of `IS NOT NULL` in SQL?

help.concept: `IS NOT NULL` checks whether a field does contain a real value.
help.hint_2: It is used when rows where the chosen column is not missing are needed.

options.a: It checks whether a value is present

m4_s03_null_mistake_meaning.title: What is a common NULL mistake?
m4_s03_null_mistake_meaning.prompt: Which of these is a common NULL mistake?

help.concept: A common mistake is using normal equality style with NULL instead of NULL-specific checks.
help.hint_2: NULL is special and should be checked with NULL-specific syntax.

m4_s03_null_mistake_meaning.hint: Think wrong way of comparing NULL.

options.a: Trying to compare NULL with normal equality style
options.d: Running the query after writing it

m4_s03_null_result_check.title: How should you check a NULL result?
m4_s03_null_result_check.prompt: After running a query that checks for missing data, what is a strong habit?

help.concept: You should verify that the returned rows really do have missing or present values where expected.
help.hint_1: Think check the actual returned fields.
help.hint_2: Do not assume the logic worked. Confirm that the target field is actually missing or actually present.

m4_s03_null_result_check.hint: Check the target field in the returned rows.

options.a: Verify that the target field is really missing or present as intended
options.d: Assume the NULL logic worked automatically

m4_s03_null_true.title: What is true about NULL?
m4_s03_null_true.prompt: Select all true statements about `NULL` in SQL.

help.concept: `NULL` means missing or unknown data and must be treated specially.
help.hint_1: Think missing value, not regular text or number.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about absent data and NULL-specific logic.

options.a: `NULL` means a value may be missing
options.b: `NULL` does not mean the same thing as zero
options.c: `NULL` does not mean the same thing as empty text
options.d: `NULL` is just another ordinary text value

m4_s03_is_null_true.title: What is true about IS NULL?
m4_s03_is_null_true.prompt: Select all true statements about `IS NULL` in SQL.

help.concept: `IS NULL` is the standard way to find missing values.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about correct NULL checking for absent data.

options.b: `IS NULL` is used when a field has no stored value
options.c: `IS NULL` is different from exact matching with =
options.e: `IS NULL` can be used in WHERE conditions

m4_s03_is_not_null_true.title: What is true about IS NOT NULL?
m4_s03_is_not_null_true.prompt: Select all true statements about `IS NOT NULL` in SQL.

help.concept: `IS NOT NULL` is used to keep rows where a value exists.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about finding rows with actual stored values.

options.a: `IS NOT NULL` checks for present values
options.c: `IS NOT NULL` can be used in WHERE conditions
options.d: `IS NOT NULL` means partial text search
options.e: `IS NOT NULL` is useful when only rows with actual data are needed

m4_s03_null_mistakes_true.title: What are common NULL mistakes?
m4_s03_null_mistakes_true.prompt: Select all common mistakes related to NULL handling.

help.concept: NULL mistakes usually come from treating NULL like normal text or number data.
help.hint_1: Think wrong comparison style and wrong assumptions.
help.hint_2: Choose the mistakes that show confusion about missing-value logic.

m4_s03_null_mistakes_true.hint: Think treating NULL like normal data.

options.a: Trying to treat NULL like a normal exact-match value
options.b: Using the wrong kind of check for missing data
options.c: Failing to verify that the returned rows really have missing values
options.d: Using `IS NULL` to find missing values
options.e: Assuming NULL behaves like ordinary text

m4_s03_missing_data_workflow_true.title: What matches the right workflow?
m4_s03_missing_data_workflow_true.prompt: Select all statements that match a good workflow for missing-data checks.

help.concept: A good workflow is to decide whether missing or present values are needed, choose the correct NULL check, run the query, and verify the result.
help.hint_2: Choose the steps that help connect the missing-data goal to the correct SQL check.

m4_s03_missing_data_workflow_true.hint: Decide missing or present first, then verify it.

options.a: Decide whether the field should be missing or present
options.b: Use `IS NULL` or `IS NOT NULL` to match that meaning
options.c: Run the query and inspect the returned field values
options.d: Check that the rows actually fit the missing-data rule
options.e: Ignore the returned field values after running

m4_s03_reorder_null_meaning.title: What NULL means
m4_s03_reorder_null_meaning.prompt: Reorder the ideas to explain NULL.

help.concept: NULL means a value is missing or unknown.
help.hint_2: It is not the same as zero or empty text.

tokens.t1: `NULL` means no actual value is stored there
tokens.t2: it often means missing or unknown data
tokens.t3: it is different from zero or empty text

m4_s03_reorder_is_null_logic.title: How IS NULL works
m4_s03_reorder_is_null_logic.prompt: Put the missing-value idea in order.

help.concept: IS NULL is the correct way to find missing values.
help.hint_2: Then think the special check.

tokens.t1: sometimes a column has no stored value
tokens.t2: `IS NULL` checks for that missing value directly
tokens.t3: rows with missing values stay in the result

m4_s03_reorder_is_not_null_logic.title: How IS NOT NULL works
m4_s03_reorder_is_not_null_logic.prompt: Reorder the ideas to explain present-value checks.

help.concept: IS NOT NULL keeps rows where a value exists.
help.hint_2: Then think missing rows are excluded.

tokens.t1: sometimes rows where a value is present are needed
tokens.t2: `IS NOT NULL` checks for an existing value
tokens.t3: rows with actual stored values stay in the result

m4_s03_reorder_null_mistake_logic.title: Avoid NULL mistakes
m4_s03_reorder_null_mistake_logic.prompt: Put the mistake-avoidance idea in order.

help.concept: NULL needs special checks instead of normal equality-style comparisons.
help.hint_2: Then choose the correct NULL-specific check.

tokens.t1: NULL is not handled like ordinary text or numbers
tokens.t2: normal equality-style checking can cause mistakes
tokens.t3: use IS NULL or IS NOT NULL instead

help.concept: A good workflow is decide missing or present, choose the check, run, verify.
help.hint_2: Verification comes last.

tokens.t1: decide whether the field should be missing or present
tokens.t2: use IS NULL or IS NOT NULL to match that meaning
tokens.t3: run the query and verify the returned field values

m4_s03_fill_null_word.title: Meaning of NULL
m4_s03_fill_null_word.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best word.

help.concept: NULL often means the value is missing.
help.hint_2: The answer is missing.

m4_s03_fill_is_null_keyword.title: Missing-value check

help.concept: IS NULL is used to check for missing values.
help.hint_2: The answer is IS NULL.

m4_s03_fill_is_null_keyword.template: The SQL phrase used to find missing values is ____.

m4_s03_fill_is_not_null_keyword.title: Present-value check
m4_s03_fill_is_not_null_keyword.prompt: Fill in the blank with the correct SQL phrase.

help.concept: IS NOT NULL is used to keep rows with present values.
help.hint_2: The answer is IS NOT NULL.

m4_s03_fill_is_not_null_keyword.template: The SQL phrase used to find present values is ____.

m4_s03_fill_null_comparison_warning.title: Common NULL mistake
m4_s03_fill_null_comparison_warning.prompt: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.

help.concept: A common mistake is treating NULL like normal equality-style data.
help.hint_2: The word is equality.

m4_s03_fill_null_comparison_warning.template: A common NULL mistake is using normal ____ style with NULL.

m4_s03_fill_missing_data_check.title: Opposite of missing
m4_s03_fill_missing_data_check.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best word.

help.concept: IS NOT NULL is used when the value is present.
help.hint_2: The answer is present.

m4_s03_fill_missing_data_check.template: Use `IS NOT NULL` when the field should be ____.
